org.docgene.help

Class AbstractHelpContentViewer<C,W>

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.docgene.help.AbstractHelpContentViewer<C,W>
  • Type Parameters:
    C - the help component Class
    W - the help dialog window Class
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    HelpContentViewer<C,W>
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    JFXHelpContentViewer, SwingHelpContentViewer

    public abstract class AbstractHelpContentViewer<C,W>
extends java.lang.Object
implements HelpContentViewer<C,W>
    The abstract class for the HelpContentViewer. The HelpContentViewer allows to show the content of a help system. The steps to use to use the HelpContentViewer are:
    • Install the ArticlesModel by calling the HelpContentViewer.installModel(org.docgene.help.model.ArticlesModel) method. Note that this can also be performed directly through the JavaHelpFactory by the JavaHelpFactory.install(org.docgene.help.HelpContentViewer) method
    • Get the HelpContentViewer component: HelpContentViewer.getHelpComponent() and add it in your GUI
    • Or alternatively get the HelpContentViewer dialog window: HelpContentViewer.getHelpWindow(java.lang.Object, java.lang.String, int, int) and add it in your GUI
    Adding context-sensitive help on components is performed through the HelpContentViewer.registerContextHelp(java.lang.String, java.lang.Object) method.

      • setIcon

        public void setIcon(java.lang.String buttonID,
                    java.net.URL url)
        Set the URL for the icon for a button. The button id can be:
        • HelpContentViewer.ICON_HOME for the home button
        • HelpContentViewer.ICON_FORWARD for the forward button
        • HelpContentViewer.ICON_BACKWARD for the backward button
        • HelpContentViewer.ICON_GLOSSARY for the glossary button
        Specified by:
        setIcon in interface HelpContentViewer<C,W>
        Parameters:
        buttonID - the button id
        url - the URL
